name: observability
title: Choose the observability stack
description: Pick logging, metrics, and tracing tools. Decide what's emitted, retention windows, and who has access.

starter.summary: Decide how production behavior is observed and debugged.
starter.issue: Observability gaps don't matter until they do — usually at 2am during an incident. Decide the stack and bake it in from day one.
constraints: Budget (Datadog and Honeycomb get expensive quickly).
Team's comfort with structured logs vs unstructured.

positions.title: Datadog (all-in-one SaaS)
positions.description: APM, logs, metrics, errors in one place.

positions.title: Honeycomb (event-based, tracing-first)
positions.description: High-cardinality observability. Excellent for distributed systems debugging.
pros: BubbleUp is unmatched for outlier finding

positions.title: Platform-native + Sentry for errors
positions.description: Use the hosting platform's built-in logs/metrics (Vercel, Fly, CloudWatch) plus Sentry for error tracking.
pros: Cheap or free at small scale

implications: Need OpenTelemetry instrumentation in code (or vendor SDK).
Log retention policy must be set.
Alerting tool and on-call rotation need to follow.
Cost-budget for the chosen tool needs to be tracked.

notes_for_agent: For early-stage projects with no budget, 'platform-native + Sentry' is almost always right. For projects expecting >$1k/mo infra spend or running distributed services, recommend a real observability tool early. Ask about expected request volume and whether the team has been burned by missing telemetry before.
